profiles.area: South Bali / Bukit Peninsula concentration — Jimbaran (HQ), Uluwatu/Pecatu (Bingin, Padang Padang), Ungasan, plus Kerobokan, Sanur, Nusa Penida, and an expansion portfolio in Yogyakarta (Java). Sources: bukitvista.com/area/jimbaran, /area/uluwatu, TripAdvisor listings in Pecatu/Nusa Penida, Crunchbase ("across the island and Yogyakarta").
profiles.management_model: Full-service property management on a revenue-share model: "Business" package at 15% revenue share and "Inspired Investment" at 20%, zero fixed monthly fees, commission identical across booking channels (OBSERVED via search snippets of bukitvista.com/pricing, /blog/villa-management-fee-2025, /blog/bukit-vistas-commission-2025). Also offers property valuation, business-plan and onboarding services ("go live in under 14 days").

Range 170-180+, cross-confirmed by 3 sources.
profiles.price_segment: Budget-to-mid market. Example rates: private room at Bingin Beach ~$127/night (OBSERVED, Airbnb via search snippet), long stays from $790/month (OBSERVED, bukitvista.com property snippet), Despacito Loft rated 4/5 on TripAdvisor as "Specialty Inn". Portfolio includes guesthouses, bungalows, lofts, tree-houses, not just villas (LinkedIn company page).
profiles.direct_booking: Mixed OTA-first model: own WordPress site lists properties at bukitvista.com/property/* with "View On Airbnb" links pushing guests to Airbnb (OBSERVED via Google-indexed snippets); direct bookings also exist (owner docs state "direct bookings are settled 1-3 days post booking" — OBSERVED). Guest enquiries funnel through WhatsApp/phone handled by GAIA, their in-house OpenAI-powered Guest AI Assistant that "handles queries, manages reservations, and streamlines arrival coordination" 24/7 (OBSERVED: bukitvista.com/gaia, /blog/guest-communication-bukit-vista-2026). Post-booking, guests use a self-service portal at guest.bukitvista.com. NOT TESTED: exact wa.me hrefs, prefilled text, whether villa/dates carry into WhatsApp — bukitvista.com returns HTTP 403 to non-browser fetchers and the session egress proxy denied raw curl, so page HTML could not be inspected.
profiles.booking_engine: No third-party PMS/booking engine (Guesty, Lodgify, etc.) detected; evidence points to a custom in-house stack on WordPress: guest portal (guest.bukitvista.com), internal tool bigrr.bukitvista.com, "BV GO" app (bukitvista.com/bv-go-app), GAIA/RAIA AI assistants built by their own engineering team (bukitvista.com/gaia; ZoomInfo lists an "AI Full-Stack Engineer" employee). profiles.notes: Company verifiably exists and is highly active in July 2026 (April-2026 blog posts, 2026 market reports, active LinkedIn). BUT it is a poor Villa Ops OS prospect: Bukit Vista is arguably the most tech-forward manager in Bali and has ALREADY BUILT the exact product in-house — GAIA (Guest AI Assistant: OpenAI-powered WhatsApp first responder that auto-collects structured guest requirements, manages reservations, runs 24/7) and RAIA (Reservation AI Assistant), plus a centralized multi-channel inbox across Airbnb/WhatsApp/direct, a guest portal, and an internal engineering team that co-develops with university interns (Telkom University GAIA project). Selling them a $5-9.5k external enquiry system duplicates their core competency and brand story ('data-driven hospitality innovation'). They fit the surface profile (10+ villas, WhatsApp, own site, reservations staff, identifiable decision maker Jing Cho Yang) but fail on need. Recommend deprioritizing as a sales target — better used as a competitor benchmark, or approached for a niche gap (e.g. owner-lead/B2B enquiry pipeline rather than guest enquiries), and choosing a less tech-mature 10-30-villa operator as the actual buyer. Research caveat: bukitvista.com serves HTTP 403 to non-browser fetchers and this session's egress proxy denied raw curl to all external hosts, so every official-site fact was captured from search-engine-indexed snippets of official pages (classified OBSERVED) rather than direct page loads; HTML-level checks (wa.me hrefs, prefill text, booking-engine scripts) are NOT TESTED. No forms were submitted and no one was contacted.

profiles.notes: METHOD CAVEAT: this sandbox's egress policy blocked ALL direct page fetches (proxy answered 403 to curl CONNECT for every host incl. archive.org; WebFetch 403 even for example.com), so all site facts were gathered via WebSearch snippets of official/third-party pages — treat official-site facts as OBSERVED-via-search, not HTML-verified. NOT TESTED: wa.me hrefs/prefill, booking-engine scripts, form handlers, Instagram recency, Google Maps rating; re-run the HTML-level audit from an unrestricted network before outreach. DISAMBIGUATION: 'Gravity Bali Hotel' (Pecatu, TripAdvisor d10512504) and 'Gravity Eco Boutique Hotel' are unrelated businesses — do not use their reviews. A snippet about 'slow communication' complaints came from a competitor's generic blog (balimanagement.villas), not attributable to Gravity Bali; no Gravity-specific negative reviews surfaced. Company verifiably operates in July 2026 (April 2026 press, 2026 blog posts, 2026 directory). FIT RATIONALE (9/10): 35–40+ premium villas; direct bookings pushed with perks but fulfilled by a manual form→marketing-team→quote flow; WhatsApp-centric guest ops with published sub-5-minute/sub-1-hour response promises and a self-contradictory weekend concierge gap; identifiable owner-operators (Olivier & Celine Cancé); hiring sales/office staff. Pitch angle: Villa Ops OS protects their published response-time promises 24/7 (incl. weekends), carries villa/date context from website into WhatsApp, and lifts direct-vs-OTA conversion. Deduction: boutique 'personal touch' identity may create resistance to AI-assisted first response — frame as augmenting, not replacing, their concierges.
